positive.literal: single object, centered, clean plain background, no characters, product shot

negative.literal: people, characters, hands, cluttered background, scene, multiple objects
person, human, face, mannequin, model, body, worn by person

laneRules: item is a SINGLE object on a clean plain background — an icon that composites later. No characters, no hands, no scene.
Covers weapons / armor / materials / clothing / decor (decor folded into item in v2).

OWNER FIX (v2) — WEARABLES SHOW NO PERSON: clothing/armor are a flat-lay empty garment. 'person, human, face, mannequin, model, body, worn by person' are hard-negated (a face appeared in a cloak shot until this was added). Learned live.

ERA LAW (all entity lanes) — a clothing/gear slot MUST carry an era qualifier (e.g. 'medieval fantasy clothing'); without it the model defaults to modern dress (a 'wool robe' rendered as a bathrobe). mapNpcToSlots injects the world's era descriptor (world.era) into the attire slot when the world data carries one; a world with no era field is a WORLD-DATA GAP (reported, never invented here).
